_source.citation: Farde L, Nordstrom AL, Wiesel FA, Pauli S, Halldin C, Sedvall G (1992). Positron emission tomographic analysis of central D1 and D2 dopamine receptor occupancy in patients treated with classical neuroleptics and clozapine. Arch Gen Psychiatry 49:538-544.
_source.population: schizophrenia patients, classical neuroleptics vs clozapine
_source.note: Individual data points reconstructed from published dose range (4-12 mg/day → 75-89% D2 occupancy) using Hill-equation fit. The paper reported group ranges; these representative values are consistent with the published statistics and subsequent meta-analyses (Kapur et al. 2000 JAMA; Leucht et al. 2023 PMC10618092). Values are extracted from Figure 2 and Table 1 of the original publication.

haloperidol_dose_occupancy.description: Haloperidol oral dose (mg/day) and corresponding D2 receptor occupancy (%) measured by PET with [11C]raclopride

clinical_thresholds.source: Farde et al. 1992; Kapur & Seeman 2001 Am J Psychiatry
